content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import {
23	  selectExercisesScaled,
24	  selectSectionsScaled,
25	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
26	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
27	import {
28	  validateExerciseStructural,
29	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
30	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
31	  type StructuralFailure,
32	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
33	import {
34	  validateExerciseSemantic,
35	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
36	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
37	import {
38	  RouterStrategy,
39	  type VariationStrategy,
40	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
41	
42	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
43	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
44	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
45	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
46	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
47	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
48	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
49	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
50	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
51	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
52	
53	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
54	
55	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
56	
57	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
58	export interface StrategyResult {
59	  exerciseId: string
60	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
61	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
62	}
63	
64	/**
65	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
66	 *
67	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
68	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
69	 *
70	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
71	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
72	 * @param subject     Duplication subject
73	 * @param payload     Payload CMS instance
74	 * @param aiStrategy  Optional AI strategy override (for testing)
75	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
76	 */
77	export async function runStrategy(
78	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
79	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
80	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
81	  payload: Payload,
82	  aiStrategy?: VariationStrategy,
83	): Promise<StrategyResult> {
84	  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload, undefined, aiStrategy)
85	  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
86	
87	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
88	  return {
89	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
90	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
91	    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
92	  }
93	}
94	
95	/**
96	 * Cap source-exercise block count to match the structural validator's
97	 * `TOO_MANY_SECTIONS` ceiling (5). Pure: returns a NEW exercise object with
98	 * a trimmed `content.blocks` array when needed, otherwise the original.
99	 *
100	 * Picks 5 representative blocks from the source via `selectSectionsScaled` —
101	 * the same scaling-random bucketing the lesson-level selector uses. Preserves
102	 * source order, so the variation reads like a natural shorter version of the
103	 * original rather than a random shuffle.
104	 *
105	 * Rationale: without this, a 10-block source always failed validation
106	 * (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS is a blocking failure → exercise dropped from output).
107	 * Admins doing deep variations on real lessons would silently lose any
108	 * exercise that exceeded the block cap.
109	 */
110	function trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise: ExerciseDoc): ExerciseDoc {
111	  const blocks = exercise.content?.blocks
112	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length <= 5) return exercise
113	  const picked = selectSectionsScaled(blocks, 5)
114	  logger.info(
115	    { exerciseId: exercise.id, originalCount: blocks.length, trimmedTo: picked.length },
116	    'Source exercise has more than 5 blocks — trimming via scaling-random selector',
117	  )
118	  return {
119	    ...exercise,
120	    content: { ...exercise.content, blocks: picked },
121	  }
122	}
123	
124	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
125	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
126	  switch (code) {
127	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
128	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
129	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
130	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
131	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
132	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
133	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
134	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
135	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
136	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
137	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
138	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
139	      return 'regenerate'
140	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
141	      return 'regenerate'
142	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
143	      return 'skip'
144	    default:
145	      return 'skip'
146	  }
147	}
148	
149	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
150	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
151	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
152	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
153	// before deleting this assert.
154	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
155	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
156	void _concurrencyAssert
157	
158	/**
159	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
160	 *
161	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
162	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
163	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
164	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
165	 */
166	async function appendEntry(
167	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
168	  payload: Payload,
169	  duplicationId: string,
170	  exerciseRef: string,
171	  sectionIndex: number,
172	  code: string,
173	  message: string,
174	): Promise<void> {
175	  const action = suggestAction(code)
176	  try {
177	    const current = await payload.findByID({
178	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
179	      id: duplicationId,
180	      depth: 0,
181	      overrideAccess: true,
182	    })
183	    await payload.update({
184	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
185	      id: duplicationId,
186	      data: {
187	        [bucket]: [
188	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
189	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
190	          {
191	            exerciseRef,
192	            sectionIndex,
193	            code,
194	            message,
195	            suggestedAction: action,
196	            resolved: false,
197	          },
198	        ],
199	      } as never,
200	      overrideAccess: true,
201	    })
202	  } catch (err) {
203	    logger.error(
204	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
205	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
206	    )
207	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
208	  }
209	}
210	
211	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
212	async function appendFailure(
213	  payload: Payload,
214	  duplicationId: string,
215	  exerciseRef: string,
216	  sectionIndex: number,
217	  code: string,
218	  message: string,
219	): Promise<void> {
220	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
221	}
222	
223	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
224	async function appendWarning(
225	  payload: Payload,
226	  duplicationId: string,
227	  exerciseRef: string,
228	  sectionIndex: number,
229	  code: string,
230	  message: string,
231	): Promise<void> {
232	  return appendEntry('warnings', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
233	}
234	
235	/**
236	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
237	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
238	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
239	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
240	 */
241	async function appendOutputExercise(
242	  payload: Payload,
243	  duplicationId: string,
244	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
245	): Promise<void> {
246	  try {
247	    const current = await payload.findByID({
248	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
249	      id: duplicationId,
250	      depth: 0,
251	      overrideAccess: true,
252	    })
253	    const existing =
254	      (current as unknown as { outputExercises?: OutputExerciseMapping[] }).outputExercises ?? []
255	    await payload.update({
256	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
257	      id: duplicationId,
258	      data: { outputExercises: [...existing, mapping] } as never,
259	      overrideAccess: true,
260	    })
261	  } catch (err) {
262	    logger.error(
263	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef: mapping.sourceExerciseId },
264	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',
265	    )
266	  }
267	}
268	
269	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
270	type ExerciseDoc = {
271	  id: string
272	  content?: { blocks?: ContentBlock[] }
273	}
274	
275	/** Mapping entry from source exercise to generated output exercise. */
276	interface OutputExerciseMapping {
277	  sourceExerciseId: string
278	  outputExerciseId: string
279	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
280	}
281	
282	/**
283	 * Create the output lesson (draft) by deep-copying every source-lesson field.
284	 *
285	 * Earlier versions only copied title/slug/status/chapter/course/tenant/locale,
286	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
287	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
288	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
289	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
290	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
291	 */
292	async function createOutputLesson(
293	  payload: Payload,
294	  sourceLessonId: string,
295	  level: string,
296	): Promise<string> {
297	  const source = await payload.findByID({
298	    collection: 'lessons',
299	    id: sourceLessonId,
300	    depth: 0,
301	    overrideAccess: true,
302	  })
303	  const sourceData = source as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
304	  // Drop Payload-managed + lineage fields; the rest carries over.
305	  //   slug:           regenerated by formatSlugAsync from the new title.
306	  //   blocks:         references source exercise IDs; rebuilt by the exercise
307	  //                   auto-add hook as new variation exercises are saved.
308	  //   translatedFrom: this is a fresh variation, not a translation copy.
309	  //   createdBy:      set to the duplicating admin via createdByField hook.
310	  const {
311	    id: _id,
312	    createdAt: _c,
313	    updatedAt: _u,
314	    slug: _ignoreSlug,
315	    blocks: _ignoreBlocks,
316	    translatedFrom: _ignoreTranslatedFrom,
317	    createdBy: _ignoreCreatedBy,
318	    ...rest
319	  } = sourceData as Record<string, unknown> & {
320	    id?: unknown
321	    createdAt?: unknown
322	    updatedAt?: unknown
323	    slug?: unknown
324	    blocks?: unknown
325	    translatedFrom?: unknown
326	    createdBy?: unknown
327	  }
328	  void _id
329	  void _c
330	  void _u
331	  void _ignoreSlug
332	  void _ignoreBlocks
333	  void _ignoreTranslatedFrom
334	  void _ignoreCreatedBy
335	  const base = (rest.title as string) ?? 'Lesson'
336	  const newLesson = await payload.create({
337	    collection: 'lessons',
338	    data: {
339	      ...rest,
340	      title: `${base} - Variation (${level})`,
341	      status: 'draft', // never publish a duplicate by default
342	    } as never,
343	    overrideAccess: true,
344	  })
345	  return newLesson.id
346	}
347	
348	/**
349	 * Create a draft exercise in the output lesson with the generated blocks.
350	 * Returns the mapping entry for tracking.
351	 */
352	async function createOutputExercise(
353	  payload: Payload,
354	  result: StrategyResult,
355	  outputLessonId: string,
356	): Promise<OutputExerciseMapping> {
357	  const ex = await payload.create({
358	    collection: 'exercises',
359	    data: {
360	      title: `Variation of ${result.exerciseId}`,
361	      lesson: outputLessonId,
362	      content: { blocks: result.blocks },
363	      status: 'draft',
364	    } as never,
365	    overrideAccess: true,
366	  })
367	  return {
368	    sourceExerciseId: result.exerciseId,
369	    outputExerciseId: ex.id,
370	    strategy: result.strategy,
371	  }
372	}
373	
374	/**
375	 * Process a single exercise through the duplication pipeline.
376	 *
377	 * Steps:
378	 *  1. Run strategy → get new exercise content
379	 *  2. Structural validation → collect failures
380	 *  3. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script' → semantic validation
381	 *  4. On any failure: stream failure to DB, return null
382	 *  5. On all pass: return StrategyResult for the orchestrator to use
383	 */
384	async function processExercise(
385	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
386	  duplicationId: string,
387	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
388	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
389	  payload: Payload,
390	  aiStrategy?: VariationStrategy,
391	): Promise<StrategyResult | null> {
392	  const exerciseRef = exercise.id
393	
394	  // Pre-trim source blocks. The structural validator caps exercises at 5
395	  // blocks (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS — renderer / UX limit). Source exercises with
396	  // 9-10 blocks would always fail variation. We sub-sample to 5 using the
397	  // same scaling-random selector that picks exercises from a lesson: pick 1
398	  // block per evenly-spaced bucket across the source, seeded for
399	  // reproducibility. Preserves source order, so the variation feels like a
400	  // natural truncation of the original.
401	  const trimmedExercise = trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise)
402	
403	  // Step 1: Run strategy
404	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
405	  try {
406	<<<<<<< HEAD
407	    strategyResult = await runStrategy(exercise, level, subject, payload, aiStrategy)
408	=======
409	    strategyResult = await runStrategy(trimmedExercise, level, subject, payload)
410	>>>>>>> origin/dev
411	  } catch (err) {
412	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
413	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
414	    await appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
415	    return null
416	  }
417	
418	  // Step 2: Structural validation. Split failures into blocking (drop the
419	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
420	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
421	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
422	  //
423	  // We pass the source exercise's blocks (post-trim) so the validator can
424	  // suppress MISSING_QUESTION when the source itself had an empty prompt
425	  // (e.g. legacy geometry exercises where the question is in the figure, not
426	  // as text). Otherwise every such variation would falsely fail.
427	  const sourceBlocks = (trimmedExercise.content?.blocks ?? []) as ContentBlock[]
428	  const structuralFailures: StructuralFailure[] = validateExerciseStructural(
429	    strategyResult.blocks,
430	    sourceBlocks,
431	  )
432	  const blockingFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
433	  const warningFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => !BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
434	
435	  // Record blocking failures and non-blocking warnings into separate buckets
436	  // so the review UI can show them under different headings.
437	  for (const failure of blockingFailures) {
438	    await appendFailure(
439	      payload,
440	      duplicationId,
441	      exerciseRef,
442	      failure.blockIndex ?? 0,
443	      failure.code,
444	      failure.message,
445	    )
446	  }
447	  for (const warning of warningFailures) {
448	    await appendWarning(
449	      payload,
450	      duplicationId,
451	      exerciseRef,
452	      warning.blockIndex ?? 0,
453	      warning.code,
454	      warning.message,
455	    )
456	  }
457	
458	  if (blockingFailures.length > 0) {
459	    return null
460	  }
461	
462	  // Warning-only path: fill placeholders so the exercise renders. Warnings are
463	  // already recorded so the admin can find the TODOs in the review screen.
464	  if (warningFailures.length > 0) {
465	    strategyResult = {
466	      ...strategyResult,
467	      blocks: fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders(strategyResult.blocks),
468	    }
469	  }
470	
471	  // Step 3: Semantic validation. Skipped for:
472	  //  - level=none (deep clone, no AI variation to judge)
473	  //  - script strategy (deterministic, no hallucination risk)
474	  //  - warning-only path (we just inserted "_TODO: hint not provided by AI_"
475	  //    placeholders; a semantic reviewer would flag those as nonsensical and
476	  //    demote an exercise that should ship with a polish-later flag into a
477	  //    hard failure)
478	  if (strategyResult.strategy !== 'script' && level !== 'none' && warningFailures.length === 0) {
479	    const semanticResult = await validateExerciseSemantic(
480	      strategyResult.blocks,
481	      level,
482	      strategyResult.strategy,
483	      payload,
484	    )
485	    if (!semanticResult.ok) {
486	      await appendFailure(
487	        payload,
488	        duplicationId,
489	        exerciseRef,
490	        0,
491	        SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
492	        `Semantic mismatch: ${semanticResult.reasons.join('; ')}`,
493	      )
494	      return null
495	    }
496	  }
497	
498	  return strategyResult
499	}
500	
501	/**
502	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
503	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
504	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
505	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
506	 */
507	const FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS = 30_000
508	
509	/**
510	 * Default assumed worst-case time for one exercise (pass-1 + pass-2 +
511	 * validators on gemini-3.1-pro-preview). Used to decide whether the next
512	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
513	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
514	 */
515	const ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS = 300_000
516	
517	export type RunOutcome = 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' | 'failed' | 'in_progress'
518	
519	export interface RunOptions {
520	  /**
521	   * Absolute wall-clock deadline (Date.now() ms). The orchestrator returns
522	   * `in_progress` as soon as the remaining time is too short to fit another
523	   * exercise — leaving the record in `running` so the next cron tick picks
524	   * up where this one left off. Default: no deadline (process all).
525	   */
526	  deadlineMs?: number
527	}
528	
529	/**
530	 * Orchestrate the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record.
531	 * Resumable: skips exercises already processed in previous ticks and streams
532	 * each new output mapping to the DB as it completes. A Vercel function
533	 * timeout mid-run leaves a clean partial state — the next cron tick continues
534	 * from there.
535	 *
536	 * @param duplicationId  ID of the LessonDuplications record to process
537	 * @param payload        Payload instance
538	 * @param aiStrategy     Optional AI strategy override (for testing — inject a mock/stub)
539	 * @param options.deadlineMs  Absolute timestamp by which to stop processing
540	 *
541	 * Pre-condition: record has status `pending` (fresh) or `running` (resuming).
542	 * Post-condition (returned outcome):
543	 *  - 'succeeded'   — all picked exercises produced an output, 0 failures
544	 *  - 'needs_review'— all picked exercises terminal, ≥1 failure
545	 *  - 'in_progress' — deadline hit, more work to do, status left as 'running'
546	 *  - 'failed'      — fatal error before any exercises processed
547	 */
548	export async function runDuplicationOrchestrator(
549	  duplicationId: string,
550	  payload: Payload,
551	  aiStrategy?: VariationStrategy,
552	  options: RunOptions = {},
553	): Promise<RunOutcome> {
554	  // Load the duplication record
555	  const duplication = await payload.findByID({
556	    collection: 'lesson-duplications',
557	    id: duplicationId,
558	    depth: 1, // need sourceLesson relationship
559	    overrideAccess: true,
560	  })
561	
562	  if (!duplication) {
563	    logger.error({ duplicationId }, 'LessonDuplications record not found')
564	    return 'failed'
565	  }
566	
567	  // Already terminal — nothing to do.
568	  if (
569	    duplication.status === 'succeeded' ||
570	    duplication.status === 'needs_review' ||
571	    duplication.status === 'failed'
572	  ) {
573	    logger.warn(
574	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
575	      'LessonDuplications record already terminal, skipping',
576	    )
577	    return duplication.status as RunOutcome
578	  }
579	
580	  // Accept both 'pending' (first tick) and 'running' (resuming) — anything
581	  // else is unexpected.
582	  if (duplication.status !== 'pending' && duplication.status !== 'running') {
583	    logger.warn(
584	      { duplicationId, status: duplication.status },
585	      'LessonDuplications record in unexpected status, skipping',
586	    )
587	    return 'failed'
588	  }
589	
590	  try {
591	    const sourceLessonId =
592	      typeof duplication.sourceLesson === 'string'
593	        ? duplication.sourceLesson
594	        : (duplication.sourceLesson as { id?: string })?.id
595	
596	    if (!sourceLessonId) {
597	      throw new Error('sourceLesson relationship is missing or invalid')
598	    }
599	
600	    // First tick: create output lesson, flip to running. Resuming tick:
601	    // reuse the existing output lesson recorded on the record.
602	    let outputLessonId: string
603	    const existingOutputLessonRef = duplication.outputLesson as
604	      | string
605	      | { id?: string }
606	      | null
607	      | undefined
608	    const existingOutputLessonId =
609	      typeof existingOutputLessonRef === 'string'
610	        ? existingOutputLessonRef
611	        : existingOutputLessonRef?.id
612	
613	    if (duplication.status === 'pending') {
614	      outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
615	        payload,
616	        sourceLessonId,
617	        duplication.level ?? 'light',
618	      )
619	      await payload.update({
620	        collection: 'lesson-duplications',
621	        id: duplicationId,
622	        data: { status: 'running', outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
623	        overrideAccess: true,
624	      })
625	    } else {
626	      // resuming
627	      if (!existingOutputLessonId) {
628	        // Shouldn't happen — running record without an outputLesson means a
629	        // previous tick crashed before the initial write. Treat as fresh.
630	        outputLessonId = await createOutputLesson(
631	          payload,
632	          sourceLessonId,
633	          duplication.level ?? 'light',
634	        )
635	        await payload.update({
636	          collection: 'lesson-duplications',
637	          id: duplicationId,
638	          data: { outputLesson: outputLessonId } as never,
639	          overrideAccess: true,
640	        })
641	      } else {
642	        outputLessonId = existingOutputLessonId
643	      }
644	    }
645	
646	    const allExercises = await getSourceExercisesForLesson(payload, sourceLessonId)
647	
648	    // No artificial cap — resumability handles long runs across multiple
649	    // cron ticks. selectExercisesScaled still bounds at 20 per the original
650	    // product spec (admin scaling-random across the whole lesson).
651	    const PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP = 20
652	    const selectedExercises = selectExercisesScaled(
653	      allExercises as ExerciseDoc[],
654	      PER_RUN_EXERCISE_CAP,
655	    )
656	
657	    // Filter out exercises already processed on prior ticks. Sources of truth:
658	    //  1. The record's `outputExercises` — explicitly recorded successes.
659	    //  2. The record's `failures` — terminal per-exercise failures.
660	    //  3. Output exercises already present in the output lesson itself, even
661	    //     if their mapping never made it back into `outputExercises`. This
662	    //     closes the crash-window between createOutputExercise (writes the
663	    //     exercise to Mongo) and appendOutputExercise (records the mapping):
664	    //     if the function dies between those two calls, the exercise is
665	    //     durably saved but the record doesn't know about it; without this
666	    //     check the next tick would re-process the source and create a
667	    //     duplicate. We identify the source by the deterministic title
668	    //     `Variation of <sourceExerciseId>` set in createOutputExercise.
669	    const existingOutputExercises = await payload.find({
670	      collection: 'exercises',
671	      where: { lesson: { equals: outputLessonId } },
672	      limit: 0,
673	      depth: 0,
674	      overrideAccess: true,
675	    })
676	    const titleSourceIds = new Set<string>()
677	    const orphanMappings: OutputExerciseMapping[] = []
678	    const knownMappingSources = new Set(
679	      ((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
680	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
681	      ),
682	    )
683	    for (const ex of existingOutputExercises.docs as Array<{
684	      id: string
685	      title?: string
686	    }>) {
687	      const match = typeof ex.title === 'string' ? /^Variation of (\S+)/.exec(ex.title) : null
688	      if (match) {
689	        titleSourceIds.add(match[1])
690	        // Recover the mapping for any orphan exercise (created but never
691	        // recorded). Next tick start, we'll reconcile by appending these
692	        // mappings so the record matches reality going forward.
693	        if (!knownMappingSources.has(match[1])) {
694	          orphanMappings.push({
695	            sourceExerciseId: match[1],
696	            outputExerciseId: ex.id,
697	            strategy: 'ai',
698	          })
699	        }
700	      }
701	    }
702	
703	    // Reconcile orphan mappings recovered from the output lesson so the
704	    // duplications record reflects reality before this tick continues.
705	    for (const orphan of orphanMappings) {
706	      await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, orphan)
707	      logger.warn(
708	        { duplicationId, sourceExerciseId: orphan.sourceExerciseId },
709	        '[orchestrator] Recovered orphan output exercise (crash-window mapping)',
710	      )
711	    }
712	
713	    const processedIds = new Set<string>([
714	      ...((duplication.outputExercises as unknown as OutputExerciseMapping[]) ?? []).map(
715	        (m) => m.sourceExerciseId,
716	      ),
717	      ...((duplication.failures as unknown as Array<{ exerciseRef: string }>) ?? []).map(
718	        (f) => f.exerciseRef,
719	      ),
720	      ...titleSourceIds,
721	    ])
722	    const remaining = selectedExercises.filter((ex) => !processedIds.has(ex.id))
723	
724	    logger.info(
725	      {
726	        duplicationId,
727	        totalPicked: selectedExercises.length,
728	        alreadyProcessed: selectedExercises.length - remaining.length,
729	        remaining: remaining.length,
730	      },
731	      'Orchestrator tick start',
732	    )
733	
734	    const duplicationLevel = duplication.level as 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep'
735	    const duplicationSubject =
736	      (duplication.subject as DuplicationSubject | null | undefined) ?? 'mixed'
737	
738	    // Process exercises one at a time (CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 — appendEntry's
739	    // read-modify-write isn't safe under parallel writes; see compile-time
740	    // guard). Check deadline between exercises and bail out if the next one
741	    // can't fit, returning `in_progress` so the next tick continues.
742	    for (const exercise of remaining) {
743	      if (options.deadlineMs !== undefined) {
744	        const remainingMs = options.deadlineMs - Date.now()
745	        if (remainingMs < FINALIZE_BUDGET_MS + ASSUMED_EXERCISE_DURATION_MS) {
746	          logger.info(
747	            { duplicationId, remainingMs, remainingExercises: remaining.length },
748	            'Orchestrator tick yielding before deadline — leaving record running',
749	          )
750	          return 'in_progress'
751	        }
752	      }
753	
754	      const result = await processExercise(
755	        exercise,
756	        duplicationId,
757	        duplicationLevel,
758	        duplicationSubject,
759	        payload,
760	        aiStrategy,
761	      )
762	      if (result === null) continue // failure already recorded by processExercise
763	
764	      try {
765	        const mapping = await createOutputExercise(payload, result, outputLessonId)
766	        // Stream the success immediately so a function timeout doesn't lose
767	        // this exercise — the actual exercise doc is already in MongoDB
768	        // (createOutputExercise just created it), this just keeps the
769	        // duplications record in sync.
770	        await appendOutputExercise(payload, duplicationId, mapping)
771	      } catch (err) {
772	        const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown create error'
773	        logger.error(
774	          { exerciseRef: exercise.id, err },
775	          'createOutputExercise failed — exercise dropped from output lesson',
776	        )
777	        await appendFailure(
778	          payload,
779	          duplicationId,
780	          exercise.id,
781	          0,
782	          GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE,
783	          `Failed to save variation: ${message}`,
784	        )
785	      }
786	    }
787	
788	    // All selected exercises terminal: finalize status. Re-read failures
789	    // count from the record since we streamed entries during the run.
790	    const finalRecord = await payload.findByID({
791	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
792	      id: duplicationId,
793	      depth: 0,
794	      overrideAccess: true,
795	    })
796	    const finalFailures = (finalRecord.failures as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
797	    const finalOutputs =
798	      (finalRecord.outputExercises as unknown as unknown[] | undefined)?.length ?? 0
799	    const finalStatus: 'succeeded' | 'needs_review' =
800	      finalFailures === 0 ? 'succeeded' : 'needs_review'
801	
802	    logger.info(
803	      {
804	        duplicationId,
805	        total: selectedExercises.length,
806	        succeeded: finalOutputs,
807	        failed: finalFailures,
808	        finalStatus,
809	      },
810	      'Duplication orchestrator completed',
811	    )
812	
813	    await payload.update({
814	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
815	      id: duplicationId,
816	      data: { status: finalStatus } as never,
817	      overrideAccess: true,
818	    })
819	    return finalStatus
820	  } catch (err) {
821	    logger.error({ duplicationId, err }, 'Orchestrator run failed')
822	    await payload.update({
823	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
824	      id: duplicationId,
825	      data: { status: 'failed' },
826	      overrideAccess: true,
827	    })
828	    throw err
829	  }
830	}
831

394	  // Pre-trim source blocks. The structural validator caps exercises at 5
395	  // blocks (TOO_MANY_SECTIONS — renderer / UX limit). Source exercises with
396	  // 9-10 blocks would always fail variation. We sub-sample to 5 using the
397	  // same scaling-random selector that picks exercises from a lesson: pick 1
398	  // block per evenly-spaced bucket across the source, seeded for
399	  // reproducibility. Preserves source order, so the variation feels like a
400	  // natural truncation of the original.
401	  const trimmedExercise = trimSourceBlocksIfNeeded(exercise)
402	
403	  // Step 1: Run strategy
404	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
405	  try {

407	  } catch (err) {
408	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
409	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
410	    await appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
411	    return null
412	  }
413

415	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
416	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
417	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
